agenda.expected: Preview and carry unfinished dated work with one atomic vault transaction.
agenda.fixture: Create yesterday/today disposable journal fixtures with one unfinished item, preview the carry, commit it once, rerun to prove idempotency, then transactionally remove both fixtures.

autoresearch.expected: Complete one bounded read-only protected web research flow and file validated output.
autoresearch.fixture: Research the definition of Python len at exactly https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#len through the protected fetcher. Pass only the research request and that exact URL as the protected topic; keep acceptance cleanup instructions in the coordinator. Synthesize networklessly into a bounded disposable note set, validate provenance before cleanup, then remove every filed product page and every generated product-index link; leave runner-owned temp, append-only bookkeeping, and state cleanup to the runner.

backlog.expected: Add, list, and promote one disposable backlog item without duplicate writes.
backlog.fixture: Add a uniquely named disposable backlog item, confirm it appears once, promote it into a disposable decision page, confirm the inbox transition, then remove both artifacts transactionally.

canvas.expected: Create and validate one disposable Obsidian canvas artifact.
canvas.fixture: Create a uniquely named two-node disposable canvas with one valid edge, validate its JSON and referenced note, then remove the canvas and note.

clarify.expected: Ask one material question at a time and make no repository mutation.
clarify.fixture: For the request 'Improve the pipeline' with no scope or success criterion, produce exactly the first material clarifying question plus a recommended answer and rationale. The question itself is the observable result: do not wait for a human reply; verify the checkout stayed unchanged and finish the cell.

close.expected: Save and terminate the agent process without closing its cmux surface.
close.fixture: In a nested disposable cmux surface, start a harmless runtime session with a disposable save target. Launch every fixture command explicitly through /bin/zsh so the cmux user's interactive shell cannot reinterpret zsh syntax. Invoke close, prove the agent process exited while that exact surface remained open, then close only the nested fixture surface.

daily.expected: Produce one grounded disposable daily summary through the runtime-specific bounded agent.
daily.fixture: Create one disposable same-day session record and one local commit as evidence, generate today's summary, rerun to prove deterministic replacement, then remove the summary and all fixture evidence.

defuddle.expected: Clean one bounded public page into readable Markdown without unrelated content.
defuddle.fixture: Fetch and clean the official Python documentation index through the protected path. If defuddle-cli is unavailable, apply and verify the documented fallback cleanup instead of accepting raw WebFetch Markdown: remove project navigation and footer boilerplate while retaining main content. Validate before removing the disposable output; leave append-only bookkeeping to the runner.

dispatch.expected: Start one isolated task worktree and deliver its approved plan exactly once.
dispatch.fixture: Dispatch an approved plan that adds one line to a uniquely named disposable text fixture, using only the worktree root supplied by LLM_OBSIDIAN_WORKTREES. Prove exact task/worktree identity and a single handoff, then finish and remove task product output; leave the runner-owned nested worktree root and registry for runner cleanup without merging.

distill-runbook.expected: Create a human-executable runbook from sanitized disposable command evidence.
distill-runbook.fixture: Record two harmless disposable shell commands, distill them into one uniquely named copy-paste runbook, verify no secret-like or agent-only content, then remove the runbook and command evidence.

draft.expected: Return alternative redacted drafts without sending external communication.
draft.fixture: Draft two concise alternatives declining a fictional meeting because of a schedule conflict. Include no real names or addresses, send nothing, verify the checkout stayed unchanged, and finish after presenting the drafts.

find-session.expected: Return ranked prior-session candidates without mutating the vault.
find-session.fixture: Create three disposable session metadata/history fixtures where two mention a unique task token, query that token, verify deterministic top-two ranking and read-only behavior, then remove the fixtures.

journal.expected: Create and update disposable dated journal data atomically.
journal.fixture: Create a disposable journal entry for today, add one reminder and one completed task in a single transaction, verify provenance and stable rerun behavior, then remove the entry transactionally.

learn.expected: Run one bounded study and quiz interaction grounded in disposable learning notes.
learn.fixture: Create a tiny disposable learning note defining idempotency, present one grounded study summary and one quiz question with its expected answer. Do not wait for a human reply; verify read-only interaction after fixture setup, then remove the note.

obsidian-bases.expected: Create and validate one disposable Obsidian Base.
obsidian-bases.fixture: Create two disposable notes and one Base that filters them by a unique tag and shows a formula column, validate the Base syntax/result set, then remove all three artifacts.

obsidian-markdown.expected: Produce valid Obsidian-flavoured Markdown with links and properties.
obsidian-markdown.fixture: Create one disposable note with required frontmatter, a wikilink, callout, embed, tag, highlight, and math example; validate syntax and link resolution, then remove it and its disposable linked note.

reap-send.expected: Validate and deliver one typed task summary callback exactly once.
reap-send.fixture: Inside a nested disposable task surface under LLM_OBSIDIAN_WORKTREES, construct a valid typed Wiki Summary with task metadata, deliver it once to the exact coordinator, prove duplicate delivery is rejected or idempotent, then remove every fixture-created scratch path; leave only runner-owned nested worktree and registry state.

reap.expected: Collect the typed task summary and finalize the approved lifecycle state.
reap.fixture: Create one nested disposable completed task under LLM_OBSIDIAN_WORKTREES with a valid typed summary, reap it into a disposable wiki page, verify provenance and exact task finalization, then remove product pages; leave runner-owned worktree and lifecycle registry cleanup to the runner.

review-dispatch.expected: Complete an opposite-runtime review and bounded same-session verification loop.
review-dispatch.fixture: Create a tiny disposable script change in a task under LLM_OBSIDIAN_WORKTREES with one intentional defect, start an opposite-runtime review, receive typed findings, fix the defect, verify in the same reviewer lane, and drive approval to finish. Remove fixture-created product/review scratch; leave runner-owned worktree, operation registry, and append-only archive bookkeeping to the runner.

review-send.expected: Submit one typed product-read-only reviewer result exactly once.
review-send.fixture: Inside a nested active reviewer lane, inspect a tiny disposable script with one known issue, submit one typed resolve result without editing the product, prove exact operation identity and duplicate safety, then clean the nested lane.

save-plan.expected: Persist one disposable plan without executing it.
save-plan.fixture: Save a uniquely named three-step disposable plan, verify required frontmatter/provenance and that none of its steps ran, then remove the plan transactionally.

save.expected: Persist and merge one disposable structured note through vault-write.
save.fixture: Save a uniquely named disposable decision note, save a second compatible detail that must merge into it rather than duplicate it, then verify provenance, one-page tiling, and vault validity before cleanup. Move the page transactionally to a bounded trash path and delete that trash copy; treat resulting append-only log/hot/index entries as runner-owned disposable bookkeeping.

unsafe-research.expected: After one warning, run one bounded single-context web scenario on the exact current session route without a second model run.
unsafe-research.fixture: After exactly one explicit unsafe single-context warning, read one stable fact from the official Python documentation, cite the page, prove no second model session or external write occurred, and finish in the current session.

wiki-fold.expected: Preview and commit one deterministic disposable operation-log fold.
wiki-fold.fixture: Create the minimum disposable operation-log entries for one fold, preview twice to prove deterministic output, commit once, prove the second commit is a no-op, then remove the fold page and fixture ledger state.

wiki-ingest.expected: Ingest one local fixture and one bounded protected URL with provenance and deduplication.
wiki-ingest.fixture: Ingest a uniquely named local Markdown fixture and the official Python documentation index through the protected URL path, rerun both for deduplication, verify provenance, then remove pages and raw artifacts.

wiki-lint.expected: Run the complete disposable-vault health check and produce deterministic findings.
wiki-lint.fixture: Create a disposable orphan, dead wikilink, and frontmatter gap, run the complete lint twice, verify deterministic findings/dashboard behavior, then remove fixtures and restore any generated dashboard.

wiki-query.expected: Answer from hybrid retrieval and from an explicitly degraded sparse fallback.
wiki-query.fixture: Create a tiny three-note corpus with one uniquely answerable question, query with hybrid retrieval, disable embeddings explicitly and query again, verify the visible degradation notice plus correct sparse answer, then remove the corpus.

wiki.expected: Route one supported wiki authoring request to the correct workflow.
wiki.fixture: Request a uniquely named Obsidian note containing a callout and wikilink, verify routing to the authoring workflow and valid output, then remove the note and its disposable link target.
